label: Music, Sound & Culture

sources.title: Computational Exploration of Magical and Divinatory Language

app_prompts: Jazz improvisation explainer: user picks a jazz standard and traces the chord changes, the modes available over each chord, and how improvisers navigate the form — bebop, modal, free.
Jazz history timeline: user picks a decade and sees the key recordings, the musicians, the venues, the social context — how jazz evolved from New Orleans through bebop, cool, free, and fusion.
Critical listening guide: user picks a jazz recording and gets a framework for hearing it — instrumentation, form, soloists, rhythm section interaction, how it fits its historical moment.
Music and society case study: user picks a musical genre and traces its social context — who made it, who consumed it, how it was received by the mainstream, what it meant to its community.

Improvisation traditions comparison: user picks two improvisation traditions (jazz vs. Indian classical, free jazz vs. gamelan) and sees how each approaches melody, rhythm, and the individual-ensemble relationship.
